reasoning: Start by increasing A to observe if Y changes in relation to A.
Y increased from 60.0 to 90.0 when A was increased from 2.0 to 3.0. This suggests Y is positively correlated with A.
Y increased to 180.0 when B was increased from 10.0 to 20.0, while A and C remained constant. This suggests Y is positively correlated with B as well.
